title: Aureline provider callback / event envelope, dedup key, import session, imported snapshot, and callback-deny audit-event vocabulary
description: Boundary schema for the typed envelope every inbound provider callback, webhook, event delivery, and inspect-only refresh is wrapped in before it touches local state. Frozen in docs/providers/provider_mode_contract.md. The contract composes with (and does not replace) the ADR-0010 browser-handoff packet and approval-ticket vocabularies; it adds the envelope, dedup key, import-session, imported-snapshot, and callback-deny records every provider-linked surface and every publish-later drain reads. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw webhook bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary; records carry opaque refs, fingerprints, and structured fields only. Five record kinds are exported: provider_callback_envelope_record (the canonical envelope), callback_dedup_key_record (a dedup fingerprint registry entry), import_session_record (one inspect_only session), imported_provider_snapshot_record (one fetched provider-side object snapshot, grouped under an import session), and callback_deny_audit_event_record (an observable deny event on the provider_handoff audit stream). The eventual provider-mode crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new callback_class, dedup_scope_class, staleness_class, import_partial_class, resolution_state_class, or deny_reason is additive-minor and requires a callback_envelope_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

callback_envelope_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this callback-envelope vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / queue-review rollups / admin-reconciliation consoles. Raw URLs, raw tokens, and raw callback bodies never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

reviewable_sentence.description: Short human-readable sentence safe to show in preview, queue-review, and export review surfaces.

provider_actor_class.description: Provider-actor class reused from schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json. A generic 'Connected' state is forbidden on every product, CLI, support-export, review-overlay, queue-review, and audit surface; 'unknown_actor_class' is repair-only and MUST NOT be collapsed with any other class.

trust_posture.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state the envelope, session, or snapshot was accepted under.

redaction_class.description: Declared redaction class reused from ADR-0010 and ADR-0007 redaction posture. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw webhook bodies, raw delegated-token bodies, and raw provider payloads never cross this boundary regardless of class.

replay_posture.description: Replay posture reused from the browser-handoff packet. The listener enforces single-use consumption, bounded-reuse counters, or read-only refresh accordingly.

callback_class.description: Frozen callback / event class vocabulary. Auth-flow return callbacks stay on the auth-callback packet in schemas/auth/auth_callback_state.schema.json; only provider-object callbacks land here.

intent_signature_status.description: Result of intent-signature / webhook-signature validation against the originating packet or subscription. Only 'verified' or 'unsigned_expected' (for subscription classes that contractually carry no signature) progress past envelope validation.

staleness_class.description: Freshness classification at the time the envelope is validated or the snapshot is consumed. 'bounded_stale' must carry a freshness_floor_ref; 'unbounded_stale' carries an acknowledged staleness rationale.

import_partial_class.description: Partial-vs-full class for an imported snapshot. Bounded partial snapshots name the scoping (labels, path prefix, page range); unbounded partial snapshots MUST visibly degrade rather than masquerade as authoritative remote truth.

deny_reason.description: Deny reason drawn from the ADR-0010 frozen set. Silent retry and silent downgrade are forbidden; every denied envelope emits a callback_deny_audit_event_record.

audit_event_id.description: Frozen subset of the ADR-0010 provider_handoff audit-event ids this contract references. No new event id is introduced by this contract.

dedup_scope_class.description: Scope the dedup key is computed against. Two envelopes that agree on dedup_scope_class and scope_ref are the same delivery; the listener refreshes freshness and MUST NOT mutate local state twice.

object_identity.description: Provider-side identity of the object the envelope, queue item, or snapshot targets. Extends the ADR-0010 object-identity set with package_version and registry_entry so the artifact-registry surface class renders without a separate vocabulary.

return_anchor.description: Stable anchor for returning the user to the originating Aureline object. Extends the ADR-0010 set with publish_later_queue_anchor and imported_snapshot_anchor so queue-review and inspect-only surfaces round-trip without query-string dependencies.

origin_disclosure.description: Canonical Aureline origin the envelope, session, or snapshot is validated against. Reused from the ADR-0010 browser-handoff packet.

policy_context.description: Policy context the envelope, session, or snapshot is validated under. A policy-epoch roll between issue and validation re-evaluates the envelope; silent continued-use is forbidden.

freshness_block.description: Structured freshness block. bounded_stale MUST carry a freshness_floor_ref; unbounded_stale MUST carry a staleness_rationale.

bounded_reuse_counter.description: Bounded-reuse counter. Present only when replay_posture is bounded_reuse.

callback_dedup_key_record.description: Dedup-key registry entry. Two envelopes that agree on dedup_scope_class + scope_ref are the same delivery; the listener updates last_observed_at and observed_delivery_count but MUST NOT mutate local state twice.

allOf.description: bounded_partial_snapshot MUST carry a partial_scope_summary naming the scoping (labels, path prefix, page range).

imported_provider_snapshot_record.description: One inspect_only snapshot of a provider-side object. Grouped under an import_session_record. Every surface that renders imported provider state (queue review, support export, admin reconciliation, inspect pane) MUST carry a snapshot_ref and render actor_scope, trust_posture, freshness, and partial_class; a stale or partial snapshot MUST visibly degrade rather than masquerade as authoritative.

inbound_envelope_refs.description: Envelopes that refreshed this session (import_session_refresh callbacks).

snapshot_refs.description: Snapshot records grouped under this session.

import_session_record.description: One inspect_only import session. Groups imported_provider_snapshot_records so imported state carries one consistent actor_scope, trust_posture, and freshness across everywhere it is rendered.

originating_browser_handoff_packet_ref.description: Opaque ref to the minting browser_handoff_packet_record when callback_class is provider_callback_return or publish_later_drain_result. Empty string for unsolicited webhook classes (which still MUST name their subscription_ref).

originating_publish_later_queue_item_ref.description: Opaque ref to the publish_later_queue_item_record the envelope closes when callback_class is publish_later_drain_result.

originating_import_session_ref.description: Opaque ref to the import_session_record the envelope refreshes when callback_class is import_session_refresh.

subscription_ref.description: Opaque ref to the provider subscription / webhook registration the envelope was received under. Required when callback_class is provider_event or provider_webhook.

audit_event_refs.description: Opaque refs to the provider_handoff audit events emitted for this envelope (browser_handoff_callback_validated / browser_handoff_callback_rejected / deferred_publish_queue_drained / deferred_publish_queue_rejected / policy_epoch_rolled_invalidations).

allOf.description: bounded_reuse_counter MUST be present when replay_posture is bounded_reuse, and MUST be absent otherwise.
provider_callback_return MUST cite its originating browser-handoff packet.
publish_later_drain_result MUST cite its originating queue item.
import_session_refresh MUST cite its originating import session.
provider_event and provider_webhook callbacks MUST name their subscription_ref.
An envelope that carries denied_at MUST name a deny_reason, and vice versa.

provider_callback_envelope_record.description: Typed envelope wrapping one inbound provider callback, webhook, event, inspect_only refresh, publish-later drain result, or provider-side retract. Raw URLs, raw callback bodies, raw webhook bodies, and raw delegated tokens never appear on the envelope; consumers resolve refs at the narrowest possible projection boundary.

repair_hook_ref.description: Opaque ref to the repair hook the surface routes to (account reselection, browser recovery, step-up authenticator, admin reconciliation). Empty string only when no repair is applicable.

callback_deny_audit_event_record.description: An observable deny event on the provider_handoff audit stream when an envelope fails validation. The envelope id, provider id, actor class, deny reason, replay posture, policy epoch, and trust state are carried; raw callback bodies, raw URLs, and raw delegated tokens MUST NOT appear. Silent retry is forbidden; the repair_hook_ref is the only reopen path.
